words.def: the act of making something that has been officially decided start to happen or be used
words.example: the implementation of the new system

words.def: to put somebody in a prison or another place from which they cannot escape
words.example: be imprisoned (for something), They were imprisoned for possession of drugs.

words.def: the act of putting somebody in a prison or another place from which they cannot escape; the state of being there
words.example: to be sentenced to life imprisonment for murder

words.def: the fact of not being able to do something
words.example: the government’s inability to provide basic services

words.def: not enough; not good enough
words.example: inadequate supplies

words.def: not suitable or appropriate in a particular situation
words.example: inappropriate behaviour/language

words.def: the extent to which something happens or has an effect
words.example: an area with a high incidence of crime

words.def: wanting to do something
words.example: She was inclined to trust him.

words.def: the fact of including somebody/something; the fact of being included
words.example: His inclusion in the team is in doubt.

words.def: if you incur something unpleasant, you are in a situation in which you have to deal with it
words.example: She had incurred the wrath of her father by marrying without his consent.

words.def: a sign that shows you what something is like or how a situation is changing
words.example: The economic indicators are better than expected.

words.def: a sign that a system, society, etc. is very bad or very wrong
words.example: The poverty in our cities is a damning indictment of modern society.

words.def: coming from a particular place and having lived there for a long time before other people came there; relating to, belonging to or developed by these people
words.example: the indigenous peoples/languages of an area

words.def: to persuade or influence somebody to do something
words.example: Nothing would induce me to take the job.

words.def: to allow yourself to have or do something that you like, especially something that is considered bad for you
words.example: indulge in something, They went into town to indulge in some serious shopping.

words.def: the unfair difference between groups of people in society, when some have more wealth, status or opportunities than others
words.example: We need to tackle inequality of opportunity wherever we find it.

words.def: well known for being bad or evil
words.example: a general who was infamous for his brutality

words.def: a baby or very young child
words.example: a nursery for infants under two

words.def: to make a disease or an illness spread to a person, an animal or a plant
words.example: infect somebody/something (with something), It is not possible to infect another person through kissing.

words.def: to make somebody/something suffer something unpleasant
words.example: inflict something on/upon somebody/something, They inflicted a humiliating defeat on the home team.

words.def: having a lot of influence on somebody/something
words.example: a highly influential book

words.def: that is a basic or permanent part of somebody/something and that cannot be removed
words.example: the difficulties inherent in a study of this type

words.def: to prevent something from happening or make it happen more slowly or less frequently than normal
words.example: A lack of oxygen may inhibit brain development in the unborn child.

words.def: to make something begin
words.example: to initiate legal proceedings against somebody

words.def: to put a drug or other substance into a person’s or an animal’s body using a syringe
words.example: inject something (into yourself/somebody/something), Adrenaline was injected into the muscle.

words.def: an act of injecting somebody with a drug or other substance
words.example: to give somebody an injection

words.def: the fact of a situation being unfair and of people not being treated equally; an unfair act or an example of unfair treatment
words.example: We are committed to fighting against poverty and injustice.

words.def: one of the people living in an institution such as a prison or a psychiatric hospital
words.example: The jail has 500 inmates.

words.def: the act of putting something inside something else; a thing that is put inside something else
words.example: An examination is carried out before the insertion of the tube.

words.def: a person who knows a lot about a group or an organization, because they are part of it
words.example: The situation was described by one insider as ‘absolute chaos’.

words.def: to look closely at something/somebody, especially to check that everything is as it should be
words.example: inspect something/somebody, The teacher walked around inspecting their work.

words.def: an official visit to a school, factory, etc. in order to check that rules are being obeyed and that standards are acceptable
words.example: Regular inspections are carried out at the prison.

words.def: the process that takes place when somebody sees or hears something that causes them to have exciting new ideas or makes them want to create something, especially in art, music or literature
words.example: Dreams can be a rich source of inspiration for an artist.

words.def: a natural quality that makes people and animals tend to behave in a particular way using the knowledge and abilities that they were born with rather than thought or training
words.example: She did not seem to have any of the usual maternal instincts.

words.def: connected with a large important organization, for example a university or bank
words.example: institutional investors

words.def: to tell somebody to do something, especially in a formal or official way
words.example: instruct somebody to do something, The letter instructed him to report to headquarters immediately.

words.def: important in making something happen
words.example: The Conservation Trust performs an instrumental role in the protection of rural environments.

words.def: not large, strong or important enough for a particular purpose
words.example: insufficient time

words.def: a remark or an action that is said or done in order to offend somebody
words.example: The crowd were shouting insults at the police.

words.def: complete and not damaged
words.example: Most of the house remains intact even after two hundred years.

words.def: the amount of food, drink, etc. that you take into your body
words.example: high fluid intake

words.def: being an essential part of something
words.example: Music is an integral part of the school's curriculum.

words.def: in which many different parts are closely connected and work successfully together
words.example: an integrated programme of patient care

words.def: the act or process of combining two or more things so that they work together
words.example: The aim is to promote closer economic integration.

words.def: the quality of being honest and having strong moral principles
words.example: personal/professional/artistic integrity

words.def: connected with or using a person’s ability to think in a logical way and understand things
words.example: Gifted children typically show great intellectual curiosity and a wide range of interests.

words.def: to increase in degree or strength; to make something increase in degree or strength
words.example: Violence intensified during the night.

words.def: the state or quality of being intense
words.example: intensity of light/sound/colour

words.def: involving a lot of work or activity done in a short time
words.example: an intensive language course

words.def: what you intend to do
words.example: She denies possessing the drug with intent to supply.

words.def: that allows information to be passed continuously and in both directions between a computer or other device and the person who uses it
words.example: interactive displays/video

words.def: the way a computer program presents information to a user or receives information from a user, in particular the layout of the screen and the menus
words.example: the user interface

words.def: to get involved in and try to influence a situation that should not really involve you, in a way that annoys other people
words.example: I wish my mother would stop interfering and let me make my own decisions.

words.def: the act of getting involved in and trying to influence a situation that should not really involve you, in a way that annoys other people
words.example: interference (in something), They resent foreign interference in the internal affairs of their country.

words.def: intended to last for only a short time until somebody/something more permanent is found
words.example: an interim government/measure/report

words.def: connected with the inside part of something
words.example: interior walls

words.def: located between two places, things, states, etc.
words.example: an intermediate stage/step in a process

words.def: to become involved in a situation in order to improve or help it
words.example: She might have been killed if the neighbours hadn't intervened.

words.def: action taken to improve or help a situation
words.example: calls for government intervention to save the steel industry

words.def: having a close and friendly relationship
words.example: intimate friends

words.def: very interesting because of being unusual or not having an obvious answer
words.example: These discoveries raise intriguing questions.

words.def: a person who examines a situation such as an accident or a crime to find out the truth
words.example: air safety investigators

words.def: that cannot be seen
words.example: a wizard who could make himself invisible

words.def: to mention or use a law, rule, etc. as a reason for doing something
words.example: It is unlikely that libel laws will be invoked.

words.def: the act of taking part in something or dealing with somebody
words.example: involvement in something, US involvement in European wars

words.def: showing that you really mean the opposite of what you are saying; expressing irony
words.example: an ironic comment

words.def: in a way that shows that you really mean the opposite of what you are saying; in a way that expresses irony
words.example: He smiled ironically.

words.def: the funny or strange aspect of a situation that is very different from what you expect; a situation like this
words.example: It was one of life's little ironies.

words.def: not important to or connected with a situation
words.example: totally/completely/largely irrelevant

words.def: the act of separating somebody/something; the state of being separate
words.example: geographical isolation

words.def: connected with a court, a judge or legal judgement
words.example: judicial powers

words.def: the place where two or more roads or railway lines meet
words.example: It was near the junction of City Road and Old Street.

words.def: the authority that an official organization has to make legal decisions about somebody/something
words.example: jurisdiction over somebody/something, The English court had no jurisdiction over the defendants.

words.def: that most people consider to be morally fair and reasonable
words.example: a just decision/law/society

words.def: a good reason why something exists or is done
words.example: justification for doing something, I can see no possible justification for any further tax increases.

words.def: to take somebody away illegally and keep them as a prisoner, especially in order to get money or something else for returning them
words.example: Two businessmen have been kidnapped by terrorists.

words.def: either of the two organs in the body that remove waste products from the blood and produce urine
words.example: a kidney infection

words.def: a country that has a king or queen as head of state (= official leader of the country)
words.example: the United Kingdom

words.def: a boy or young man
words.example: Things have changed since I was a lad.

words.def: a person or company from whom you rent a room, a house, an office, etc.
words.example: a buy-to-let landlord (= who buys houses and flats in order to rent them out)

words.def: something, such as a large building, that you can see clearly from a distance and that will help you to know where you are
words.example: The Empire State Building is a familiar landmark on the New York skyline.

words.def: the top part of your legs that forms a flat surface when you are sitting down
words.example: on somebody's lap, There's only one seat so you'll have to sit on my lap.

words.def: involving many people or things, especially over a wide area
words.example: Large areas of the forest will be cleared for ranching as part of a large-scale development plan.

words.def: a device that gives out light in which all the waves oscillate (= change direction and strength) together, typically producing a powerful beam of light that can be used for cutting metal, in medical operations, etc.
words.example: a laser beam

words.def: used to refer to the second of two things or people mentioned
words.example: He chose the latter option.

words.def: an area of ground covered in short grass in a garden or park, or used for playing a game on
words.example: In summer we have to mow the lawn twice a week.

words.def: a claim or complaint against somebody that a person or an organization can make in court
words.example: He filed a lawsuit against his record label.

words.def: the way in which the parts of something such as the page of a book, a website, a garden or a building are arranged
words.example: the layout of streets

words.def: a small hole that lets liquid or gas flow in or out of something by accident
words.example: a leak in the roof

words.def: a long or high jump
words.example: a leap of six metres

words.def: money or property that is given to you by somebody when they die
words.example: They each received a legacy of $5 000.

words.def: very famous and talked about a lot by people
words.example: a legendary figure

words.def: a law or a set of laws passed by a parliament
words.example: an important piece of legislation

words.def: connected with the act of making and passing laws
words.example: a legislative assembly/body/council

words.def: a group of people who have the power to make and change laws
words.example: a democratically elected legislature

words.def: for which there is a fair and acceptable reason
words.example: a legitimate grievance

words.def: very long, and often too long, in time or size
words.example: lengthy delays

words.def: sexually attracted to other women; connected with lesbians
words.example: the lesbian and gay community

words.def: not as great in size, amount or importance as something/somebody else
words.example: people of lesser importance

words.def: causing or able to cause death
words.example: She had been given a lethal dose of poison.

words.def: legally responsible for paying the cost of something
words.example: You will be liable for any damage caused.

words.def: willing to understand and respect other people’s behaviour, opinions, etc., especially when they are different from your own; believing people should be able to choose how they behave
words.example: liberal attitudes/views/opinions

words.def: the act or process of freeing a country or a person from the control of somebody else
words.example: a war of liberation

words.def: freedom to live as you choose without too many limits from government or authority
words.example: the fight for justice and liberty

words.def: to give somebody official permission to do, own, or use something
words.example: license something, The new drug has not yet been licensed in the US.

words.def: lasting or existing all through your life
words.example: Paul became his lifelong friend.

words.def: the chance of something happening; how likely something is to happen
words.example: There is very little likelihood of that happening.

words.def: an arm or a leg; a similar part of an animal, such as a wing
words.example: an artificial limb

words.def: the people who are going to take part in a particular event
words.example: an impressive line-up of speakers

words.def: of or in lines
words.example: In his art he broke the laws of scientific linear perspective.

words.def: to continue to exist for longer than expected
words.example: The faint smell of her perfume lingered in the room.

words.def: a list, especially an official or published list of people or things, often arranged in alphabetical order
words.example: a comprehensive listing of all airlines

words.def: the ability to read and write
words.example: a campaign to promote adult literacy

words.def: a large organ in the body that cleans the blood and produces bile
words.example: liver disease

words.def: a large area inside the entrance of a public building where people can meet and wait
words.example: a hotel lobby

words.def: a thick piece of wood that is cut from or has fallen from a tree
words.example: logs for the fire

words.def: a way of thinking or explaining something
words.example: I fail to see the logic behind his argument.

words.def: that has existed or lasted for a long time
words.example: a long-standing relationship

words.def: having been the particular thing mentioned for a long time
words.example: his long-time colleague

words.def: to appear as a large shape that is not clear, especially in a frightening way
words.example: A dark shape loomed up ahead of us.
